rulings.Case Title: IN RE: CHARLOTTE MIMS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Claimants Underinsured Motorist Claim
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 22, 2019, 2018, Line 1, RESPONDENT METLIFE's Motion To Dismiss Claimants Underinsured Motorist Claim. Respondent Met Life's motion to dismiss the underinsured motorist claim is DENIED. Counsel agreed in August 2016 to an open extension of deadlines for arbitration of the claim. (Sciacca Dec. Ex. C.) The controlling statute is Insurance Code §11580.2, and respondent cites no authority requiring dismissal under that law. CCP §§583.310 and 583.410 regard trials in court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for petitioner is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES LI VS. THAI MING CHIU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause To Thai Ming Chiu
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 22, 2019, 2018, Line 11, Order To Show Cause To Thai Ming Chiu. Judgment creditor Charles Li's motion for a court order for sale of a dwelling is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. Li may refile if he obtains and submits a declaration attaching an appraisal of the property by a licensed independent appraiser. (See CCP §704.780(b); California Practice Guide: Enforcing Judgments & Debts (TRG 2018) §6:756.) Judgment debtor shall cooperate with the appraiser on any inspection of the property. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the opposing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: ABDELBASSET ELAREF VS. ABLE SERVICES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 22, 2019, 2018, Line 3, DEFENDANT CROWN BUILDING MAINTENANCE CO.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. CONTINUED to March 8, 2019. Defendant has leave to file a brief of no more than 5 pages explaining whether section 301 of the LMRA preempts plaintiff's claims. Please focus on section 301 caselaw that involves FEHA, CFRA and/or FMLA claims as most cases have not found preemption in the FEHA context. (See Ramirez v. Fox Television Station, Inc. (1993) 998 F.2d 743, 748.) Defendant's brief shall be filed and served by February 27, 2019. Plaintiff has leave to file a responding brief of no more than 5 pages by March 4, 2019. All parties to provide courtesy copies of their filings with Department 302. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number.=(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: LEAH RICHARDS VS. BMW OF NORTH AMERICA LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating, Issue, And/Or Evidentiary Sanctions And An Order To Compel Defendant Bmw Of North America, Llc To Comply With The Courts Discovery Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, February 22, 2019, Line 1, PLAINTIFF LEAH RICHARDS' Motion For Terminating, Issue, And/Or Evidentiary Sanctions And An Order To Compel Defendant BMW Of North America, LLC To Comply With The Courts Discovery Order Pro Tem Judge Aaron Minnis, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted, no opposition filed. Defendant BMW of North America, LLC shall comply fully with the Court's Order entered 12/12/2018, within 5 days. Defendant BMW of North America, LLC also shall pay Plaintiff's Counsel Lemon Law Aid, Inc., sanctions in the amount of $2,500 plus $60 for costs within 5 days. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to aaron@minnisandsmallets.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: GEOFFREY ELDRIDGE VS. TURO INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Change Venue
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 22, 2019, 2018, Line 5, DEFENDANT TURO INC., TOMAS VOLEJNICEK's Motion To Change Venue. Defendants' motion to change venue is DENIED. Venue is proper in San Francisco because it is the first-named defendant's residence. (K.R.L. Partnership v. Sup. Ct. (2004) 120 Cal.App.4th 490, 504.) Witnesses will not be significantly inconvenienced, as San Francisco and San Mateo counties are contiguous, not remote from one another. I judicially note that many people daily commute between the two counties. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: HECTOR SIERRA ET AL VS. ALFRED'S LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Alfred'S Llc'S Petition To Compel Arbitration Of Individual Claims And Damages And To Stay Litigation Pending Resolution Continued Per Parties' Request
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 22, 2019, 2018, Line 9, Defendant Alfred'S Llc'S Petition To Compel Arbitration Of Individual Claims And Damages And To Stay Litigation Pending Resolution Continued Per Parties' Request. Defendants' petition to compel arbitration of all the complaint's counts save the Private Attorney General count is GRANTED and this litigation is STAYED pending the arbitration. (CCP §1281.4.) The two arbitration agreements' existence is undisputed. The agreements are not invalid because "permeated with unconscionability," as plaintiffs argue. Two sentences in the three-page single-spaced agreements - regarding "aggrieved employee" determination and a jury trial waiver - are unenforceable, but severable per the agreements' terms. (Serafin v. Balco Properties Ltd., LLC (2015) 235 Cal.App.4th 165, 183-184). Labor Code §229 is preempted by the Federal Arbitration Act, which governs these arbitration agreements. (Perry v. Thomas (1987) 482 U.S. 483, 492 .) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: HALEH VAKILI VS. DIGNITY HEALTH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Action On Grounds Of Another Similar Action Pending; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 22, 2019, 2018, Line 8, DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH's Motion To Stay Action On Grounds Of Another Similar Action Pending; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities. Plaintiff Haleh Vakili's proposed wage-and-hour class action is substantially similar to a proposed class action already being adjudicated against the same defendant in this court: Allison v. Dignity Health. Defendant's motion to stay is therefore GRANTED without prejudice to filing of a motion to lift the stay and consolidate the actions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)
